title: grid-template-columns
description: Defines the column structure of a CSS Grid. The fr unit distributes available space proportionally — 1fr means one equal share. Use it to create column layouts without doing the math yourself.
html:
1
2
3
4
5
6
challenge: Create a 3-column grid. Each column should take an equal share of the available space.

alternatives.name: repeat() shorthand
alternatives.description: Use repeat() when all columns share the same size. Easier to change the column count in one place, and cleaner when you have many columns.

alternatives.name: Auto-responsive columns
alternatives.description: Let the browser decide how many columns fit using auto-fill and minmax(). The grid reflows automatically as the container grows or shrinks — no media queries needed.

realWorldUsage: The repeat(auto-fill, minmax(200px, 1fr)) pattern (shown in Other solutions) is one of the most commonly reused CSS snippets in production — a fully responsive card grid with zero media queries. Used for product listings, image galleries, and dashboard widgets wherever items should fill available space and reflow automatically.
